title: Findings-to-Artifact Contract
description: The typed intermediate the report-synthesizer produces from surviving findings and that every output channel renders (SPEC §6d). It separates the two orthogonal axes — the deliverable genre (what the document is) and the delivery channel (how it is rendered). Blog and book are first-class channels; other channels arrive via the channels pack. Every section traces to the MIF finding @ids it draws on, and every source is a MIF-shaped citation, so the citation-integrity and citation-leak gates run uniformly across all outputs.

genre.description: The deliverable genre (exec-summary, academic, engineering, …). 'general' when no genre pack is enabled.

newsworthiness.description: The delta/newsworthiness signal the engine computes — the hook a publication pipeline needs (SPEC §6d).

finding_refs.description: The MIF finding @ids this artifact synthesizes (urn:mif:).

sections.description: Ordered body sections; the genre template defines which are required.

supports.description: MIF finding @ids this section draws on.

sources.description: MIF-shaped citations (url required), deduplicated across the finding set.
